name: Jambalaya1
recipeIngredient: 1 Head garlic, separated into cloves, peeled
1 small Bunch fresh basil (or 2 tablespoons dried)
1 small Bunch fresh oregano (or 2 tablespoons dried)
2 cups Sun-dried tomatoes, cut into pieces
2 pounds Boneless chicken, cut into 1 pound Wing-ettes (wings without the tips)
recipeInstructions:
1. Puree celery, peppers, onions, garlic, and herbs (except bay leaf) in a food processor.
2. Transfer to a large pot; add canned tomatoes, sun-dried tomatoes, chicken, bay leaves, and chile powder.
3. Cover and bring to a boil.
4. Reduce heat to medium and cook for about 35 minutes.
5. Thinly slice Andouille sausage and roast in a 350°F oven for about 15 minutes (this will render out grease).
6. Add sausage to the pot, reduce heat to a simmer, and cook for about 30 minutes.
7. Add shrimp and cook for about 5 minutes.
8. Season with salt to taste.
9. Serve over hot steamed rice.
From George Louie.
Serves 10 to 12.
PER SERVING: 495 calories, 57 g protein, 21 g carbohydrate, 24 g fat (6 g saturated), 214 mg cholesterol, 957 mg sodium, 5 g fiber.
